summary: DHH describes how watching his kid brother edit an inline skating film has sparked his own desire to get into photography. He's tempted to buy a digital camera but exercises restraint, acknowledging his pattern of buying expensive gadgets that end up collecting dust. He decides to think it over before spending the money.
key_points: Watching a sibling's creative work can be contagious and inspire new interests
DHH recognizes his own pattern of impulse-buying gadgets that go unused
Self-awareness about spending habits leads him to pause before a purchase
money_quotes: Having your kid brother editing video footage all day long can turn anyone into a wanna-bee director/photographer.
My record of buying fancy gizmos only to have them collect dust is getting near perfect.
No need to add further bodycounts to that list.
key_insight: Self-awareness about one's impulse-buying tendencies is the first step toward more disciplined spending.
